stakeholders.name: Rohan Kapoor
stakeholders.title: CEO
stakeholders.quote: We've clearly lost share to TripEase on price. Customers are switching. I want to match their cashback or we accept the volume loss. Either way I need a decision this week.
stakeholders.metric_they_watch: Weekly paid bookings and estimated market share
stakeholders.blind_spot: Does not distinguish volume from quality. Has not looked at net revenue per booking or cohort repeat rate.

stakeholders.name: Priya Nair
stakeholders.title: VP Product
stakeholders.quote: I've been saying this since July — the payment UI experiment we launched in June is the problem. Checkout-to-payment conversion dropped right when we launched it. We need to roll it back immediately.
stakeholders.metric_they_watch: Checkout-to-payment conversion and payment success rate
stakeholders.blind_spot: Experiment affected only 12% of users. iOS conversion is also down — proving it's not just the experiment. Effect size too small to explain aggregate.

stakeholders.name: Siddharth Rao
stakeholders.title: VP Growth & Marketing
stakeholders.quote: The organic traffic quality is fine — I can show you that. It's the paid acquisition channels. TripEase is flooding the market with cashback and we're attracting their spillover traffic — low-intent users who just compare prices. This is a traffic quality problem.
stakeholders.metric_they_watch: Channel-level cost per booking and booking-to-60-day-repeat rate by channel
stakeholders.what_they_want: Shift spend from performance marketing to brand building
stakeholders.blind_spot: Partially right about traffic quality — but has not acknowledged that his own campaigns created the low-quality traffic problem.

stakeholders.name: Anjali Mehta
stakeholders.title: Head of Analytics
stakeholders.quote: There was a step-change in conversion in the week of July 12. That's not a gradual seasonal drift. I need 48 hours to isolate whether it's a tracking issue, an experiment gone wrong, or a real business change. Please don't make decisions before I have the analysis.
stakeholders.metric_they_watch: Day-level funnel conversion with experiment exposure controls
stakeholders.what_they_want: 48 hours to run proper root cause analysis before any decision
stakeholders.blind_spot: Closest to the truth. Needs to move faster — leadership is already making decisions.

stakeholders.name: Vikram Shah
stakeholders.title: CFO
stakeholders.quote: I don't care about bookings. I care about contribution margin. We're spending ₹610 per booking in discounts to sustain volumes that used to be self-sustaining at ₹380. If this continues 6 more weeks, we miss Q2 by ₹180 crore. Cap the discount budget and find the real cause.
stakeholders.metric_they_watch: Contribution margin per booking and cumulative discount burn
stakeholders.what_they_want: Hard cap on discount budget; mandate root cause analysis before further spend
stakeholders.blind_spot: Financially correct diagnosis. Does not have a view on which operational lever to pull.

stakeholders.name: Deepa Krishnan
stakeholders.title: Head of Customer Support
stakeholders.quote: Payment failure contacts are up 38%. Customers are calling us saying money was deducted but booking not confirmed. This is a trust problem. Engineering needs to treat this as P1.
stakeholders.metric_they_watch: Contact rate per booking and CSAT on payment failure contacts
stakeholders.what_they_want: Escalate payment failure as P1 to engineering
stakeholders.blind_spot: Absolute contact volume up — but driven partly by more bookings attempting payment. Rate per booking also rose, but payment failure is not the primary conversion driver.
